data.title: PostToolUse hook: inject forced next-step reminder after sub-agent Task/Skill completion
data.description:
## Context

Bug analysis of 818 closed bugs identified a cross-cutting failure pattern: mid-workflow orchestrator attention loss after sub-agent Task/Skill completion. Orchestrators receive a result (e.g., REVIEW_RESULT:passed, SKILL_RESUMED) but fail to execute the next required step, going idle or bypassing the result entirely.

Example bugs: 007a-62eb (sprint idle after REVIEW_RESULT:passed, record-review skipped), 7d7a-b707 (skill completes, orchestrator doesn't resume), 8985-4efc (skill result ignored, file read directly instead), b509-0590 (sub-skill dispatch orphans caller).

This is NOT catchable by declarative manifest fields — if the orchestrator stalls, it also fails to update any field. Requires harness-level intervention: a PostToolUse hook that fires after Task/Skill returns and injects an explicit next-step reminder into orchestrator context.

Related to (but distinct from) the broader stage-boundary preconditions manifest epic. That epic catches declarative end-of-workflow gaps; this hook catches mid-workflow attention failures.

## Success Criteria

- PostToolUse hook fires after Task or Skill tool completion in orchestrator sessions
- Hook reads the sub-agent's structured signal (REVIEW_RESULT, SKILL_RESUMED, COMPLETION_VERIFIER_VERDICT, etc.) from the tool result
- Hook injects a next-step reminder into the orchestrator's context referencing the completed sub-agent type and the expected follow-up action per the skill's workflow contract
- Hook does not fire for sub-agents operating inside another sub-agent (scoped to main-session orchestrator only)
- Coverage: sprint, fix-bug, preplanning, implementation-plan, review dispatches
- Configurable via dso-config.conf (orchestration.next_step_injection.enabled, default true)

## Dependencies

- Does not block the preconditions manifest epic; they are complementary
- May inform the signal contracts required (if sub-agents need to emit structured handoff signals consistently)
